title: Prompt engineering is a $300K role. Here's how to get in
post:
Prompt engineering roles are hitting $300K at top tech companies. Here's the path in:

1. Master the fundamentals: Chain-of-thought, few-shot, and role-based prompting
2. Build a portfolio: Share your best prompts and results publicly
3. Develop domain expertise: Combine AI skills with an industry you know well
4. Learn to evaluate AI outputs: Being able to judge quality is as important as generating it
5. Understand limitations: Know when AI fails and why

The best prompt engineers I've met aren't just technical. They can articulate problems clearly and think systematically about failures.

Companies are realizing that the difference between a useful AI deployment and a wasteful one often comes down to prompt quality.

The demand is real. The money is real. The path is accessible if you're willing to put in the work.

Thoughts?
